Name: Header bar
Description: The header describes the dialogs’s task or purpose.
Dynamic Behavior: For main task dialog box, display main task title. For sub-task dialog box, display sub-task title.
Content Rules: The title should be brief and clearly describe the dialogs’s task or purpose.

Name: Content area
Description: Contains the information and/or controls needed to complete the modal’s task. It can include message text and components.
Dynamic Behavior: When the content exceed the maximum allowed vertical space, the scrollbar appears and is limited the content area only.
Error State: We should wait to validate any and all inputs when the CTA is clicked and not before.

Name: CTA bar
Description: Contains the navigational and action buttons. Action buttons, such as Save, Close, Delete, are on the right. Place the primary button—the one that guides the user toward the default action on the far right.

Name: Tertiary CTA button
Description: Place the additional CTA on the far left of the dialog box. It's not the main task action.

Name: Cancel/Back button
Description: Clicking the Cancel/Back button will close the current modal and return the user to its previous context. Cancel/Back undoes all applied changes.
Dynamic Behavior:
In the main task dialog box, the CTA is “Cancel”
In the sub-task dialog box, the CTA is “Back”. Clicking the button, go back to the main task dialog box without submitting any data. 
When all the info are submited instantly or just check the info, the primary CTA is “Done”, and there is no need for secondary CTA.

Name: Primary CTA button
Description: Clicking the primary action will complete the task.
Dynamic Behavior:
In the main task dialog box, the CTA is the action for main task. Click the button complete the task and close the dialog box.
In the sub-task dialog box, the CTA is the action for sub-task. Click the button complete the task and go back to the main task dialog box.
The primary CTA should NEVER be disabled.
